active_ingredients: Phenylephrine hydrochloride 10 mg per oral tablet (the 'PE' in many cold/sinus products, e.g., Sudafed PE)
indications_and_usage: Uses: temporarily relieves nasal and sinus congestion due to the common cold, hay fever, or other upper respiratory allergies; reduces swelling of the nasal passages and shrinks swollen membranes so you can breathe more easily. It is an oral decongestant available on the open shelf (no pharmacy-counter restriction), though oral phenylephrine's effectiveness as a decongestant has been questioned by an FDA advisory committee.
contraindications: Do not use if you are now taking, or have within the past 2 weeks taken, a prescription MAO inhibitor.
warnings_and_cautions: Ask a doctor before use if you have heart disease, high blood pressure, thyroid disease, diabetes, or trouble urinating due to an enlarged prostate. Stop use and ask a doctor if you get nervous, dizzy, or sleepless, symptoms do not improve within 7 days or are accompanied by fever. Do not exceed the recommended dose - this can raise blood pressure.
adverse_reactions: Nervousness, dizziness, headache, sleeplessness, raised blood pressure, palpitations.
drug_interactions: MAO inhibitors (hypertensive crisis); other sympathomimetics and stimulants (additive cardiovascular effects); beta-blockers (may unmask the pressor effect); some antihypertensives may be less effective.
dosage_and_administration: Adults and children 12 years and over: 10 mg every 4 hours, not more than 60 mg in 24 hours. Children 6 to under 12: 5 mg every 4 hours, not more than 30 mg in 24 hours. Children under 6: do not use.
overdosage: Overdose causes hypertension, headache, slow or irregular heartbeat (reflex), and palpitations; severe cases can cause stroke. Seek medical help; contact Poison Control (1-800-222-1222).
